## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, so this is how I'm going to set up the first round of my rapid fire testing. Remember the objective of this round is we're essentially testing out personas. This is just one way to build a lot of momentum with your campaigns for yourself or for your clients. So, we're going to be sending people straight to the sales page or the product page in this case. Now the last round of rapid fire testing, you may want to continue spending more money because it's almost an incubator for your creative. But generally speaking, you can get some pretty good data from $200 to $500 with your rapid fire test, which is pretty great because a lot of people don't have huge $2,000 budgets to test things out on. ## [09:10 - 12:14]

risky because targeting and advertising in general can be really hit and miss. So you want to, this is why you want to create many variations of ads to test and lots of different targeting options. Now when you're using campaign budget optimization feature, Facebook's going to try to feed that budget into what it is seeing as the best pocket. So it takes some of the stress out from that. Okay. Now I'm going to go ahead and create my second persona, which in this instance is agency owners as we can see here. Now when we use our second persona, we want to actually make sure that we use very similar images because the third round of rapid fire testing is essentially where you test out different creatives or basically if you test out fundamentally different visuals across your personas, when you look at your results, it might be skewed simply for the purpose that one of your images was way better than another persona's visuals. So you want to kind of make sure that the creative in terms of the imagery or videos are very similar across your personas to take that out of the equation because what we're really trying to test is who needs this the most, who's the most hungry based on our branding and our messaging and our copy, which of these personas is going to respond to us the best so that we can focus on them first and then we can test out lots of different images and videos on them.
